meaning: n.[语](修词中的)矛盾修饰法
examples: This has made many Americans conclude that business ethics is an oxymoron.
The wisdom of crowds isn't an oxymoron.
Before you ask whether that is an oxymoron, let me explain.
What we miss is that inherent is an oxymoron or paradox lays the opportunity to innovate!
To talk about a Clinton foreign policy is almost to talk about an oxymoron.
